page: Amazon Web Services r7a.large is a memory-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the Nitro hypervisor, has no local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks reveal a divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The r7a.large demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests, alongside strong results in text processing and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core performance is average to weak, as shown by the multi-core stress-ng and Geekbench scores. Memory bandwidth benchmarks are mixed, with read-only bandwidth categorized as weak, while write bandwidth and Passmark memory scores perform at an average level.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core, the instance is positioned for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel compute. It is suitable for small-scale database operations, caching layers, and lightweight text processing tasks. However, its weak performance in database operations, Redis SET operations, and multi-threaded compiling indicates that it is less suited for high-throughput transactional databases or heavy multi-threaded build pipelines. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, it offers DDR5 memory density on a dedicated AMD EPYC platform, making it a viable option for memory-bound applications with modest CPU scaling requirements.
description: Amazon Web Services r7a.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. The instance lacks local storage and integrated GPUs. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU and text processing capabilities, though multi-core performance and memory read bandwidth are relatively weak. Qualitatively, the r7a.large offers a cost-efficient entry point for memory-bound workloads that do not require extensive parallel processing. It is best suited for small-scale databases, caching layers, and lightweight text processing applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 1 Gbps network baseline, suitable for memory-bound workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and DDR5 RAM for memory-intensive, single-core-reliant workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz
16.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Strong single-core CPU and text processing performance
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for small-scale databases, caching, and memory-bound tasks
